title: SQL Developer is not working
description: SQL Developer is not working . Access denied error.
solution:
1. Close All Browsers
Save any important work.
Close all open browser windows (Chrome, Edge, Firefox, etc.).
Make sure they are fully closed:
Press Ctrl + Shift + Esc → open Task Manager
End any remaining browser processes
2. Flush DNS Cache
Press Windows + R
Type cmd and press Enter
In the Command Prompt, type:
ipconfig /flushdns
Press Enter
You should see: “Successfully flushed the DNS Resolver Cache”
3. Reset Citrix Workspace
Open Citrix Workspace
Click on your profile icon (top right corner)
Select Advanced Preferences
Click Reset Citrix Workspace
Confirm by clicking Yes / Reset
4. Clear Browser History & Cache
For Chrome / Edge:
Press Ctrl + Shift + Delete
Select:
Browsing history
Cookies and site data
Cached images and files
Choose Time range: All time
Click Clear data
5. Try to Open SQL Developer
Launch SQL Developer
Check if it opens without errors

title: SAP BI4 Launchpad not opening
description: User unable to open SAP BI Launchpad; page keeps loading or shows blank screen
solution:
1. Close All Browsers
Save any important work
Close Chrome / Edge / Firefox completely
Press Ctrl + Shift + Esc → Task Manager
End all browser-related processes

2. Clear Browser Cache
Open Chrome / Edge
Press Ctrl + Shift + Delete
Select:

* Browsing history
* Cookies and site data
* Cached images and files
  Set Time range to All time → Click Clear Data

3. Enable Pop-ups
Go to Settings → Privacy & Security → Site Settings
Click Pop-ups and redirects
Add BI Launchpad URL → Allow

4. Verify Network Connection
Disconnect and reconnect VPN
Ensure stable internet connection

5. Try Launching BI4
Open browser
Enter BI Launchpad URL
Login and verify access

title: Power BI Report Not Loading
description: Reports show blank screen or keep loading
solution:
1. Refresh the Report
Open Power BI Service
Press Ctrl + F5 for hard refresh

2. Clear Browser Cache
Press Ctrl + Shift + Delete
Select all cache options → Click Clear Data

3. Check Power BI Service Status
Open https://status.powerbi.com
Ensure no outage is reported

4. Open in Incognito Mode
Press Ctrl + Shift + N
Login and access report

5. Re-login to Power BI
Click profile → Sign out
Sign in again

title: SQL Server Connection Timeout
description: Unable to connect to SQL Server
solution:
1. Verify Server Name
Open SQL Server Management Studio (SSMS)
Check correct Server Name and Authentication type

2. Test Network Connectivity
Press Windows + R → cmd → Enter
Type:

ping <server_name>

3. Check SQL Services
Press Windows + R → services.msc
Find SQL Server (MSSQLSERVER)
Right-click → Start/Restart

4. Enable TCP/IP
Open SQL Server Configuration Manager
Go to SQL Network Configuration
Enable TCP/IP

5. Retry Connection
Open SSMS
Enter credentials and test

title: Oracle SQL Developer Connection Failed
description: Connection error while connecting to Oracle DB
solution:
1. Verify Connection Details
Open SQL Developer
Check:

* Hostname
* Port
* Service Name / SID

2. Test Connection
Click Test button

3. Check VPN Connection
Ensure VPN is connected

4. Restart Oracle Listener
Open Command Prompt
Type:

lsnrctl restart

5. Try Connecting Again
Click Connect
Verify success

title: Power BI Dataset Refresh Failed
description: Scheduled refresh is failing in Power BI
solution:
1. Open Power BI Service
Go to workspace
Click Datasets

2. Open Dataset Settings
Click More options (…) → Settings

3. Update Credentials
Scroll to Data source credentials
Click Edit credentials
Re-enter username/password

4. Check Gateway
Go to Gateway Connection
Ensure gateway is online

5. Trigger Manual Refresh
Click Refresh Now

title: SAP WebI Report Not Refreshing
solution:
1. Open Report
Login to BI Launchpad
Open Web Intelligence report

2. Switch to Edit Mode
Click Design → Edit

3. Refresh Data
Click Data Refresh button

4. Check Database Credentials
Click Data → Data Providers → Properties
Verify login details

5. Save and Re-open
Save report
Close and reopen

title: Azure Pipeline Failed
description: Pipeline execution failed in Azure DevOps
solution:
1. Open Azure DevOps
Go to project → Pipelines

2. Open Failed Pipeline
Click failed run

3. View Logs
Click failed stage
Review error message

4. Fix Issue
Update script / config
Correct credentials if needed

5. Re-run Pipeline
Click Run pipeline → Run

title: SQL Query Running Slow
solution:
1. Open Query in SSMS
Paste query

2. Enable Execution Plan
Click Include Actual Execution Plan

3. Run Query
Click Execute

4. Analyze Plan
Identify:

* Table scan
* Missing index

5. Optimize Query
Add indexes
Remove unnecessary joins
Avoid SELECT *

title: Oracle Listener Not Working
solution:
1. Open Command Prompt
Press Windows + R → cmd

2. Check Listener Status
Type:

lsnrctl status

3. Start Listener
Type:

lsnrctl start

4. Verify Configuration
Check listener.ora file

5. Retry DB Connection
Open SQL Developer → Connect

title: Power BI Gateway Offline
solution:
1. Login to Gateway Server
Remote desktop to server

2. Restart Gateway Service
Press Windows + R → services.msc
Find On-premises data gateway
Click Restart

3. Login to Gateway App
Open gateway
Sign in again

4. Check Internet Connection
Ensure server has internet

5. Verify in Power BI
Refresh gateway status

title: SAP BI User Login Failed
solution:
1. Verify Credentials
Ensure correct username/password

2. Select Authentication Type
Choose:

* Enterprise
* LDAP

3. Reset Password
Go to CMC → Users
Reset password

4. Check Account Lock
Unlock user account

5. Retry Login
Login again

title: SQL Job Failed
solution:
1. Open SSMS
Connect to SQL Server

2. Open SQL Server Agent
Expand → Jobs

3. View Job History
Right-click job → View history

4. Identify Error
Check failed step

5. Fix & Retry
Correct issue → Right-click → Start Job

title: Power BI Visuals Not Showing
solution:
1. Refresh Report
Press Ctrl + F5

2. Check Filters
Remove filters from panel

3. Verify Dataset
Check dataset refresh

4. Reconnect Data
Go to Edit → Refresh Data

5. Reopen Report
Close and reopen

title: SAP WebI Export to Excel Not Working
solution:
1. Open Report
Login to BI Launchpad

2. Click Export
Click Export → Excel

3. Allow Pop-ups
Browser → Settings → Allow pop-ups

4. Clear Cache
Press Ctrl + Shift + Delete → Clear Data

5. Try Different Browser
Use Edge or Chrome

title: Azure Pipeline Stuck in Queue
description: Pipeline is not starting and remains in queued state
solution:
1. Login to Azure DevOps
Open browser → Go to Azure DevOps portal
Navigate to your Project

2. Open Pipelines
Click Pipelines → Runs
Select the queued pipeline

3. Check Agent Availability
Click Agent Pool
Verify if agent is Online

4. Restart Agent (if required)
Login to agent machine
Restart Azure DevOps Agent Service

5. Retry Pipeline Run
Click Run pipeline → Run
Verify it starts execution

title: SQL Login Failed for User
description: User receives login failed error in SQL Server
solution:
1. Open SSMS
Launch SQL Server Management Studio

2. Verify Credentials
Enter correct:

* Username
* Password

3. Check Authentication Mode
Right-click server → Properties
Click Security
Select SQL Server and Windows Authentication Mode

4. Unlock Login
Go to Security → Logins
Right-click user → Properties
Ensure account is enabled

5. Retry Login
Reconnect using correct credentials

title: Oracle Tablespace Full
description: Database not accepting new data due to full tablespace
solution:
1. Login to Oracle DB
Open SQL Developer

2. Check Tablespace Usage
Run:

SELECT tablespace_name, used_percent FROM dba_tablespace_usage_metrics;

3. Add Datafile
Execute:

ALTER TABLESPACE users ADD DATAFILE 'users02.dbf' SIZE 500M;

4. Resize Existing Datafile
Run:

ALTER DATABASE DATAFILE 'users01.dbf' RESIZE 1G;

5. Verify Changes
Re-run usage query and confirm space is available

title: Power BI Dataset Not Found
solution:
1. Open Power BI Service
Go to app.powerbi.com

2. Navigate to Workspace
Click Workspaces → Select Workspace

3. Check Dataset Tab
Click Datasets
Confirm dataset presence

4. Verify Permissions
Click Access → Check user role

5. Republish Dataset (if missing)
Open Power BI Desktop
Click Publish → Select workspace

title: SAP BI Report Scheduling Failed
solution:
1. Login to CMC
Open Central Management Console

2. Go to Instances
Click Folders → Report → Instances

3. Check Failed Job
Open failed instance → View error

4. Verify Destination Settings
Check email/file destination

5. Reschedule Report
Click Schedule → Run Now

title: SQL Server High CPU Usage
solution:
1. Open SSMS
Connect to SQL Server

2. Identify High CPU Queries
Click Activity Monitor

3. View Processes
Sort by CPU usage

4. Kill Problem Query (if needed)
Right-click session → Kill Process

5. Optimize Query
Rewrite query / add index

title: Oracle Database Not Starting
solution:
1. Open Command Prompt
Press Windows + R → cmd

2. Connect as SYSDBA
Type:

sqlplus / as sysdba

3. Start Database
Run:

startup

4. Check Errors
Open alert log file

5. Fix Issue & Restart
Correct parameter/file issue
Run startup again

title: Power BI Login Issue
solution:
1. Open Power BI Service
Go to app.powerbi.com

2. Clear Browser Cache
Press Ctrl + Shift + Delete
Select all → Clear

3. Check Credentials
Enter correct email/password

4. Reset Password
Go to login page → Click Forgot Password

5. Try Different Browser
Use Chrome or Edge

title: SAP BI CMS Down
solution:
1. Login to Server
Remote into BI server

2. Open BI Central Configuration Manager (CCM)
Search → Open CCM

3. Check CMS Status
Locate CMS service

4. Restart CMS
Right-click → Restart

5. Start Dependent Services
Start all BI services after CMS

title: Azure Pipeline Permission Denied
solution:
1. Login to Azure DevOps
Open project

2. Open Pipeline Settings
Go to Pipelines → Select Pipeline

3. Check Security
Click … (More options) → Security

4. Grant Permissions
Add user
Select:

* Run pipeline → Allow

5. Retry Pipeline
Click Run pipeline

title: SQL Database in Recovery Mode
solution:
1. Open SSMS
Connect to SQL Server

2. Check DB Status
Expand Databases

3. Set Emergency Mode
Run:

ALTER DATABASE dbname SET EMERGENCY;

4. Repair Database
Run:

DBCC CHECKDB (dbname, REPAIR_ALLOW_DATA_LOSS);

5. Bring Database Online
Run:

ALTER DATABASE dbname SET ONLINE;

title: Power BI Report Sharing Issue
solution:
1. Open Report
Go to Power BI Service

2. Click Share
Click Share button (top right)

3. Enter User Email
Add recipients

4. Assign Permissions
Select:

* View or Edit

5. Send Invitation
Click Send
Verify access

title: SAP BI Universe Connection Error
solution:
1. Open Information Design Tool (IDT)
Launch IDT

2. Open Project
Go to Local Projects → Open Universe

3. Check Connection
Right-click connection → Test Connection

4. Update Credentials
Edit username/password

5. Publish Universe
Right-click → Publish to Repository

title: Oracle Query Returning No Data
solution:
1. Open SQL Developer
Login to DB

2. Run Basic Query
Check table:

SELECT * FROM table_name;

3. Check Filters
Remove WHERE clause

4. Validate Joins
Check joins for mismatch

5. Re-run Query
Confirm data appears

title: Azure OnePipeline Artifact Missing
description: Artifacts not available after pipeline run
solution:
1. Open Azure DevOps
Go to Pipelines → Runs

2. Select Pipeline Run
Click latest run

3. Check Artifacts Section
Scroll to Artifacts

4. Verify Publish Step
Check YAML or pipeline task:

* PublishBuildArtifacts

5. Re-run Pipeline
Click Run pipeline
